query: Why did we lower the SMB target last quarter?

context_chunks.text: Lowered Q1 SMB target from 800 to 600 after pipeline review showed coverage ratio dropped from 4.0x to 2.6x quarter over quarter.
Marketing campaign performance exceeded plan in EMEA.
Engineering team velocity improved after the on-call rotation change.

rationale: Direct mention of the SMB target reduction and the reason.

query: What was decided about the EMEA pricing change?

context_chunks.text: EMEA pricing policy raised from 8% to 12% in Q1 2026 after competitive analysis.
EMEA expansion delayed to Q3 pending headcount approval.
SMB churn rate is 6% across the trial cohort.

rationale: Pricing change is the precise topic; expansion and SMB are tangential.

query: What blockers came up in last week's check-in?

context_chunks.text: Owner flagged dependency on data team for instrumentation; same blocker as week 3.
Q2 SMB conversion improved 18% week over week.
Product launched the new onboarding flow on Monday.

rationale: Only chunk c1 mentions a blocker.

query: Why did we choose enterprise expansion over SMB depth?

context_chunks.text: Decided to pursue enterprise expansion in Q2 because SMB acquisition CAC has been climbing for three quarters and ARR per enterprise account is 12x SMB.
Hired two enterprise account executives in March.
Updated product roadmap with five new SMB features.

rationale: Captures the rationale; the other chunks are downstream actions, not the why.

query: What process changes did the Q1 retrospective recommend?

context_chunks.text: Q1 retro recommended moving check-ins from Friday to Tuesday so the week's actions can absorb the feedback.
Q1 wins included shipping the analytics rebuild ahead of plan.
Customer support backlog cleared in Q1.

rationale: Process change recommendation is in c1; c2 is a win, c3 is unrelated.

query: Has anyone flagged risk on the analytics platform OKR?

context_chunks.text: Risk Sentinel flagged analytics platform OKR at 0.65 risk score with stalled velocity in the last two check-ins.
Analytics team shipped the schema migration on schedule.
Sales team OKR is on track for Q2.

rationale: Direct match on risk flag for the analytics OKR.

query: What alternatives did we consider before settling on the current pricing strategy?

context_chunks.text: Alternatives considered: usage-based pricing (rejected as too complex for the SMB segment), seat-based pricing (chosen), and freemium with paywalls (rejected to preserve premium positioning).
Pricing committee meets every other Tuesday.
Customer interviews surfaced strong demand for self-serve.

rationale: c1 directly enumerates alternatives; c2 and c3 are tangential.

query: What customer feedback drove the onboarding flow redesign?

context_chunks.text: Three of five SMB customer interviews cited integration setup as their biggest pain point in trial.
NPS lifted from 32 to 45 in Q1.
Engineering capacity is constrained until the new hires onboard.

rationale: c1 contains the customer feedback that justified the redesign.

query: Which OKRs from last quarter missed their targets?

context_chunks.text: Enterprise expansion KR slipped because integration cost exceeded plan; closed at 0.4.
SMB conversion KR closed at 0.85 — exceeded plan.
Engineering platform migration KR closed at 0.5 — partial completion due to scope creep.

rationale: c1 and c3 both describe missed targets (below 0.7); c2 was over-plan.

query: Did we have any conversations about reorganising the engineering teams?

context_chunks.text: Engineering reorg proposal from March: split platform team into infrastructure and developer experience squads.
Engineering hired three new contributors in April.
Product team reorganised into squad-based structure in Q1.

rationale: c1 directly addresses engineering reorg; c2 is hiring (different topic); c3 is product, not engineering.
